methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $84M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
LuxWall is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $84M to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (KoBold Metals at $3.0B Series C, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B Series C, and Amogy at $700M Series C), alongside a broader comparable universe of nine private firms and one public company in the climate and energy sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $84M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage advancement, yielding $160M. Peer-median valuation across the three comparable companies produces a median of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B. These methods reflect meaningful methodological divergence: the capital-raised proxy implies a more conservative trajectory, while peer multiples suggest higher valuations consistent with downstream stage valuations in the sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $136M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median point estimate of $580M. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peer companies, burn rate and path to profitability, and stage-specific dilution expectations. The wide range reflects genuine ambiguity: comparable companies exhibit substantial valuation spread at similar stages, and LuxWall's specific financial metrics and market position relative to these peers remain undisclosed.
